ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn entry for Tahi Itik; mapped to the nearest parent class 'confection food product' as this is a traditional egg-white and coconut-milk sweet confection.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No specific FoodEx2 code exists for Tahi Itik; A0CKP (Sugar confectionery) is the closest defensible parent node given the high sugar content and confection nature of this product.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for this traditional Malaysian confection.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of Tahi Itik is approximately 30 grams, equivalent to a small handful of the irregularly shaped white dollops, as commonly served at festive occasions or as a tea-time snack.

nutrient_source.title: Coconut milk-based egg white confection — estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data (egg whites, coconut milk, sugar) and IMR Malaysia MyFCD traditional sweet analogues
nutrient_source.organization: USDA FoodData Central; IMR Malaysia MyFCD
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for Tahi Itik exists in USDA FoodData Central or IMR Malaysia MyFCD. Nutrient values were estimated by combining USDA FoodData Central data for egg whites (FDC ID 172183), coconut milk (FDC ID 169411), and white sugar (FDC ID 169655) in proportions consistent with traditional recipes, cross-referenced against IMR Malaysia MyFCD analogous traditional Malay sweets. The input AI-generated values (confidence 0.9) were used as a starting reference and are consistent with the component-based estimation. Saturated fat estimated from coconut milk fat composition (~85% saturated). Monounsaturated and polyunsaturated fat data not reliably disaggregated for this preparation; set to -1.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Sugary Confections — analogous estimate based on high-sugar coconut milk sweet (similar to dodol/halwa class); reference: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for Tahi Itik exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 75 (high) is estimated by analogy to high-sugar coconut milk confections (e.g., dodol, halwa-class sweets) documented in Atkinson et al. 2008 International Tables of GI and GL Values. The dominant glycemic driver is refined sugar (28.5g/100g). Glycemic load per 30g serving = (75 × 30.5 × 0.30) / 100 = 6.9g.

hypertension.key_factor: Moderate sodium content (110 mg/100g); high caloric density from fat and sugar
hypertension.guidance: Tahi Itik contains moderate sodium (110 mg/100g; 33 mg per 30g serving) and is unlikely to significantly raise blood pressure in isolation, but its high caloric density and sugar content warrant portion control for individuals managing hypertension with metabolic comorbidities.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very high sugar content (28.5g/100g) and high glycemic index (~75)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Tahi Itik has a high glycemic index (~75) and very high sugar content (28.5g/100g; ~8.6g per 30g serving), making it unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; consumption should be avoided or strictly limited to very small portions on rare occasions with close blood glucose monitoring.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat from coconut milk (~12.9g/100g) and high sugar content
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The high saturated fat content derived from thick coconut milk (~12.9g/100g) and elevated sugar levels in Tahi Itik may adversely affect lipid profiles and cardiovascular risk; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption and treat this as an occasional indulgence only.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Tahi Itik is a traditional Malay sweet confection originating from the east-coast Malaysian states of Kelantan and Terengganu. Its name literally means 'duck droppings' in Malay, a colloquial reference to its small, irregular white dollop shape. The confection is made primarily from egg whites — a byproduct of other traditional desserts such as Jala Mas that use only egg yolks — slow-cooked together with thick coconut milk and refined sugar over low heat until the mixture thickens into a dense, sweet, and slightly chewy texture. Pandan leaves are commonly added for fragrance, and ginger is sometimes included to neutralise the eggy aroma. Nutritionally, Tahi Itik is energy-dense at approximately 285 kcal per 100g, with a notable fat content of 15.2g (predominantly saturated fat from coconut milk) and a very high sugar content of 28.5g per 100g. Its high glycemic index (~75) makes it unsuitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or metabolic syndrome. It is gluten-free and halal. Tahi Itik is traditionally served during festive occasions such as Hari Raya and as a tea-time snack. A standard serving is approximately 30g.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for Tahi Itik exists in any approved primary source (USDA FoodData Central, IMR Malaysia MyFCD, HPB Singapore, ICMR-NIN, FAO/INFOODS SEA). Nutrient values were estimated from component-level data for egg whites, coconut milk, and sugar using USDA FoodData Central, cross-referenced against IMR Malaysia MyFCD analogous traditional Malay sweets. The AI-generated input values (confidence 0.9) were consistent with this estimation and were retained. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database class for high-sugar coconut milk confections.
notes: 1. NUTRIENT SOURCE: No exact Tahi Itik entry found in USDA FoodData Central or IMR Malaysia MyFCD. Values estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data (egg whites FDC 172183, coconut milk FDC 169411, white sugar FDC 169655) and cross-referenced with IMR Malaysia MyFCD analogous traditional Malay sweets. Input AI-generated values were consistent with this estimation and retained. 2. SATURATED FAT: Estimated at 12.9g/100g based on coconut milk fat composition (~85% saturated fat of total fat). 3. NEGATIVE SENTINEL VALUES: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid disaggregation for this traditional coconut milk and egg white preparation is not available in USDA FoodData Central or IMR Malaysia MyFCD as of 2026. cholesterol_mg set to -1: egg white cholesterol is negligible but precise value for this cooked preparation not confirmed in approved sources. water_g set to -1: moisture content for this slow-cooked reduction confection not reported in approved sources. 4. ZERO VALUES: dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 — naturally zero as the primary ingredients (egg whites, coconut milk, refined sugar) contain no dietary fibre. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is 0.0 — egg whites contain no vitamin A; confirmed negligible per USDA FoodData Central. vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0 — egg whites contain no vitamin D; confirmed negligible per USDA FoodData Central. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — no hydrogenated fats used in traditional preparation. 5. GI: Estimated by analogy to high-sugar coconut milk confections per Atkinson et al. 2008 International Tables of GI and GL Values (Diabetes Care). No direct GI measurement for Tahi Itik exists. GL per 30g serving = (75 × 30.5 × 0.30) / 100 ≈ 6.9g. 6. ONTOLOGY: No exact FoodOn or FoodEx2 code for Tahi Itik; approximate parent-class codes used with gap notes. 7. MULTILINGUAL: This is a Malaysian Malay dish; mandatory language per G6 is Malay (satisfied). Chinese, Tamil, Hindi not required for this cuisine category.
